text: CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System Surgical Technique CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System Providing an efficient, simple approach to augmenting partial- and full- thickness rotator cuff tears, the CuffMend system incorporates either ArthroFlex® dermal allograft for supplemental strength, 1,2 or autograft biceps. Insert the graft using the graft spreader and then securely fixate the graft using FiberStitch RC implants, for medial fixation and Knotless PushLock ® anchors for lateral bony fixation. Scientific literature has demonstrated that rotator cuff augmentation with acellular dermal graft can improve healing and reduce retear rates. 2 ArthroFLEX® Dermal Allograft ArthroFlex dermal allograft is a biohospitable acellular dermal allograft intended for supplemental support and covering for soft-tissue repair. 1,3,4 LifeNet Health’s patented and validated Matracell ® decellularization process renders the ArthroFlex dermal allograft acellular without compromising its biomechanical or biochemical properties. 3,4 Matracell technology removes donor DNA from the dermal matrix, while retaining its native growth factors, collagen, and elastin. 3,5 Matracell technology is validated to remove ≥97% of donor DNA and cellular content. 3 ArthroFlex dermal allograft is treated with Preservon ®, a proprietary and patented preservation technology that allows the graft to be fully hydrated at room temperature. 3 The SpeedFLEX™ implant builds upon the established ArthroFlex technology, increasing the convenience by offering presutured variations of the implant. ͸ ArthroFlex demonstrated decreased retear rates and improved patient-reported outcome scores after 1 and 2 years2 ͸ ArthroFlex dermal allograft provides supplemental support and protects the repair 4 ͸ Biomechanical testing has shown that ArthroFlex dermal allograft provides high ultimate load and suture retention strength 3,4 ͸ In a histologic analysis, ArthroFlex dermal allograft demonstrated the ability to remodel and integrate with host tissue after implantation 6 ͸ ArthroFlex dermal allograft is available in various precut sizes for use in augmentation ≥97% DNA and Cellular Content Removed Removing DNA and cellular components allows host cells to readily infiltrate and proliferate6 Intact Acellular Extracellular Matrix Provides a strong, biohospitable collagen scaffold that allows host cellular and vascular ingrowth6 Convenience Excellent handling; ready to use; room temperature storage (15-30 °C)7 Intact Extracellular Matrix Retains endogenous growth factors, elastin, matrikines, cytokines, and collagens5 ® 02 | CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System | 03 Autograft Tissue Compression System ͸ The Autograft Tissue Compression System is designed to create a graft using autologous tissue, such as biceps tendon. The graft can be used for CuffMend™ rotator cuff augmentation. ͸ The Autograft Tissue Compression System consists of a press and disposable plates. Holes in the plates can be used to help suture the graft. ͸ For details on preparing the graft, see pages 25-26. FiberStitch™ RC Implant The FiberStitch RC implant 1.5, curved or straight, with two polyester implants and 2-0 FiberWire ® sutures provides a suture-based solution to quickly and securely attach the medial edge of the graft to the rotator cuff tendon. ͸ Delivery device uses single-handed operation to deliver two soft suture implants ͸ Implant features two polyester suture implants connected with a continuous 2-0 FiberWire suture ͸ Implants can be delivered through varying graft thicknesses, including 1 mm, 2 mm, or 3 mm ArthroFlex ® dermal allografts ͸ 1.5 mm needle easily penetrates through the graft and delivers a low-profile implant ͸ Integrated depth stop for safe insertion ͸ Available in options to create either a mattress stitch or simple stitch CuffMend Implant System This convenient kit contains all the implants needed to perform the CuffMend technique. ͸ AR-19043 is ideal for use with the presutured SpeedFLEX implant ͸ AR-19042 is ideal for use with the Autograft Tissue Compression System or ArthroFlex dermal allograft CuffMend Graft Spreader The CuffMend graft spreader provides a straightforward approach to introducing a graft and positioning it over the repair site for final fixation. ͸ Low-profile design to simplify inserting the graft into the subacromial space ͸ Articulating arm effortlessly positions the graft over the repair site ͸ Versatile design for insertion through the lateral or posterior portals 3.5 mm Self-Punching PushLock® Suture Anchor The self-punching PushLock suture anchor provides efficient tensioning and knotless fixation of the graft construct. ͸ Simplified and familiar insertion technique ͸ PEEK self-punching eyelet simplifies insertion ͸ Visualize and adjust suture tension prior to anchor insertion to prevent overtensioning of the graft construct Lateral Portal Use a spinal needle to create a lateral working portal for placement of a 10 mm PassPort Button™ cannula. It is very important to make the portal as parallel as possible to the surface of the rotator cuff, resulting in a lateral portal location that will be more inferior compared to a standard lateral working portal. Optional Inferior Anterolateral Portal An auxiliary inferior anterolateral portal may also be created to introduce the graft spreader into the subacromial space. Lateral Approach for CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation Portal Placement Graft Sizing
